umm.digiquilt.view
Class TwoColorArrow

java.lang.Object
  umm.digiquilt.view.TwoColorArrow
All Implemented Interfaces:
Icon
public class TwoColorArrow
extends Object
implements Icon

A Icon drawn dynamically which points left and/or right, with a gradient of colors between the two sides.

Version:
$Revision: 1.1 $
Author:
Jason Biatek, last changed by $Author: biatekjt $ on $Date: 2009-05-28 19:19:53 $

Constructor Summary
TwoColorArrow(int width, int height, boolean pointLeft, boolean pointRight)
          Create a new TwoColorArrow, with the specified height and pointing left and/or right as specified.

 void setColors(Color left, Color right)
          Set two new colors to be used for the left and right sides of the arrow.

Constructor Detail

TwoColorArrow

public TwoColorArrow(int width,
                     int height,
                     boolean pointLeft,
                     boolean pointRight)
Create a new TwoColorArrow, with the specified height and pointing left and/or right as specified. The colors of the left and right sides default to black.

Parameters:
width -
height -
pointLeft -
pointRight -
